content.problemStatement:
It is JJ's birthday and you decide to gift him a string SSS (consisting of English alphabet only) of length NNN. But you also know that JJ does not like palindromes so you decide that none of the substrings of SSS of length ≥2\ge 2≥2 should be a palindrome.

Can you find any suitable string which can be gifted to JJ?

Recall that a string is called palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. noon\texttt{noon}noon and level\texttt{level}level are palindromic strings.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the string SSS to be constructed.


Output Format
For each test case, output a string SSS of length NNN such that none of its substrings (of length ≥2\ge 2≥2) is a palindrome.


If there are multiple strings which satisfy the condition, print any.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the required length of the string.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a string of length N consisting of lowercase English letters such that no substring of length ≥ 2 is a palindrome. Print each string on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=2 the constructed string is the first two characters of "abc...", i.e., "ab". No substring of length ≥2 is a palindrome. For N=4 the string is "abca"; its substrings of length 2 are "ab", "bc", "ca" (none palindrome), length 3 are "abc", "bca" (none palindrome), length 4 is "abca" (not palindrome).
A single character string trivially satisfies the condition because there is no substring of length ≥2.
N=5 → "abcab"; N=6 → "abcabc". Both strings avoid palindromic substrings of length ≥2.

solution.approach:
Use three distinct characters, e.g., 'a', 'b', 'c', and repeat them cyclically. The resulting infinite string "abcabc..." has no equal adjacent characters, so no palindrome of length 2 can appear. Any palindrome of length 3 would need the pattern x y x, which never occurs in the cyclic order. Any longer palindrome necessarily contains a palindrome of length 2 or 3 as a sub‑substring, therefore the constructed prefix of length N satisfies the requirement. For each test case we output the first N characters of this pattern.

Algorithm:
1. Read T.
2. For each test case read N.
3. Build a string by appending the character "abc"[i % 3] for i = 0 … N‑1.
4. Print the string.
The algorithm is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(Σ N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the output string of each test case (output itself), otherwise O(1) auxiliary.
